authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.1200 - Hazard Communication
authoritative_sources.publisher: Occupational Safety and Health Administration / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA HazCom is the primary U.S. workplace rule for shipped-container labels, SDS availability, and hazard communication records that gate chemical packaging and shipping coordination.
used_for: minimum workplace and shipment hazard-communication records
boundary between coordination and safety-approval authority

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 172 Subpart C - Shipping Papers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the primary federal source for shipping-paper content and availability requirements that determine whether hazardous-material movement may proceed.
used_for: movement gating based on required document availability

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 172 Subpart G - Emergency Response Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR provides the primary federal requirements for emergency response information that must accompany hazardous-material shipments and field escalations.
used_for: separating routing support from emergency command authority

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 172 Subpart H - Training
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the primary federal rule for hazmat employee training, recurrent training cadence, testing, and training-record retention that gates shipment preparation and routing.

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 173 - Shippers: General Requirements for Shipments and Packagings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 173 is the primary federal regulation for offeror and packaging requirements, including when hazardous-material shipments may be offered and under what package conditions.

authoritative_sources.title: Registration Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA's official registration guidance is authoritative for threshold-driven hazardous-material registration obligations and for the FMCSA safety-permit boundary relevant to some highway carriers.
used_for: refusal triggers when threshold-sensitive facts are missing

authoritative_sources.title: How to Use the Hazardous Materials Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA's official training guide is authoritative for determining which HMR sections apply, which records matter, and where hazmat packaging and shipping responsibilities begin or stop.
used_for: routing boundaries to transportation compliance or engineering owners

authoritative_sources.title: Hazardous Waste Transportation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's hazardous-waste transportation guidance is authoritative for manifest handling, e-Manifest workflow, generator and transporter responsibilities, and the boundary between routine chemical shipments and hazardous-waste movements.
used_for: routing between routine shipment and regulated waste-transfer workflows

authoritative_sources.title: ERG Mobile App
authoritative_sources.publisher: Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PHMSA's ERG guidance is an authoritative field-response reference that clarifies emergency-response information handling and the boundary between routing support and emergency decision authority.
used_for: field-evidence expectations when package condition changes
escalation scenarios tied to emergency-response information

authoritative_sources.title: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods: Model Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The UN Model Regulations are the recognized dangerous-goods packaging and transport standard that underpins UN specification packaging marks, packing-group terminology, and cross-border hazardous-goods vocabulary used by the lane.
used_for: cross-source consistency checks for package evidence

workflow_stages.name: request intake and shipment-scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify facility, product family, shipment type, package type, shipping mode, waste-versus-product posture, and whether the request remains inside packaged-chemical coordination before making any routing recommendation.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: prerequisite and system-of-record validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm which systems hold the approved SDS revision, shipping paper, package specification, training record, manifest path, carrier evidence, and release-owner map before a handoff is allowed to proceed.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

workflow_stages.name: dependency mapping and cross-team routing
workflow_stages.description: Build the dependency chain across plant shipping, warehouse, EHS, document control, ERP, maintenance, laboratory, carrier, and regulatory-reporting systems to determine whether the shipment can be routed, blocked, or escalated.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: field-condition, permit, and threshold escalation handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop routing when package damage, leaks, manifest needs, registration thresholds, training gaps, or emergency-response information requirements change whether the movement may proceed and escalate to the correct authority.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: closure confirmation and transfer verification
workflow_stages.description: Issue closure only when destination ownership, required artifact transfer, and any packaging, manifest, or incident blockers are explicit and complete.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

artifacts: work orders, shipment requests, delivery schedules, and destination instructions
current SDS revisions, shipped-container labels, shipping papers, and emergency response information
UN packaging specifications, package certification or test records, closure instructions, and overpack details
hazmat training records, owner authorization maps, carrier qualifications, and registration or permit evidence
hazardous-waste manifests, e-Manifest records, receiving-facility details, and transfer-chain notes
incident logs, spill or leak observations, damaged-package photos, safety observations, and hold or quarantine records
inventory counts, shortage reports, vendor commitments, and packaging-material availability records
systems_of_record: document control or quality management system

allowed: Classify packaged-chemical shipping requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Track dependencies, duplicated ownership, shortage risk, training gaps, manifest dependencies, and blocker state across chemical packaging and shipping systems.
Produce handoff summaries, dependency trackers, routing confirmations, logistics status summaries, shortage or vendor-risk escalations, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
escalate: Required facility, product, package, shipment, manifest, training, permit, inspection, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires hazard classification, package certification, engineering judgment, shipment release, regulator interpretation, environmental reporting approval, or legal authority.
A public baseline and tenant, carrier, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict on damaged packaging, threshold applicability, manifest handling, or release posture.
A package defect, leak, spill, incompatible residue, inspection finding, or incident changes whether the work may proceed.
refuse: Release or close shipment work without destination owner, required artifacts, or blocker resolution.
Approve package compliance, hazard classification, shipment release, return to service, or manifest signoff without explicit authority.
Direct loading work, book transportation, sign paperwork, or update systems of record as if the lane owned execution.

domain_failure_modes: work is handed off without current SDS, shipping paper, training, package-certification, or destination-owner evidence
multiple teams claim shipment ownership and the wrong carrier slot, shipment state, or release owner is committed
damaged, leaking, or incompatible packaging is treated as a routine logistics issue instead of an answer-changing safety and compliance event
hazardous-waste transfer requirements are missed and routine shipment handling is applied where manifest or receiving-facility controls are required
registration, permit, or threshold-sensitive facts are absent but the lane guesses instead of escalating
the lane is pressured into hazard classification, package approval, shipment release, engineering signoff, or emergency decision authority
tenant-specific carrier or packaging practices conflict with the public baseline and the lane improvises a proceed recommendation instead of routing for resolution

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State registration, permitting, and hazardous-waste adoption differences
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public federal baseline does not remove state-specific hazardous-material registration, permit, or hazardous-waste implementation differences. Runtime must retrieve jurisdiction and tenant escalation policy before asserting state-specific handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific release-owner sequence and carrier qualification controls
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources define the controlling evidence classes, but each tenant may split responsibility differently across plant shipping, transportation compliance, EHS, and warehouse teams. The lane therefore requires runtime retrieval of owner maps and carrier approval controls.

notes: The research keeps the lane anchored to chemical packaging and shipping coordination rather than generic dispatch or warehouse execution.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package because jurisdictional and tenant-specific differences are explicitly handled through retrieval and escalation rules.
